content.problemStatement:
Chef received NNN candies on his birthday. He wants to put these candies in some bags. A bag has KKK pockets and each pocket can hold at most MMM candies. Find the **minimum** number of bags Chef needs so that he can put every candy into a bag.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing three space-separated integers N,K,MN, K, MN,K,M.


Output Format
For each test case, print the minimum number of bags Chef needs so that he can put all the candies in one of the bags.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, K and M — the number of candies, the number of pockets in a bag and the maximum candies a pocket can hold.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of bags required.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we compute capacity = K*M and take ceil(N / capacity). The results are 1, 2, 2 and 4 respectively.
Capacity = 1*1 = 1, N = 1, so exactly one bag is enough.
Each bag holds 10*10 = 100 candies. 1000/100 = 10, so ten bags are required.

solution.approach: Each bag can hold at most K * M candies because each of its K pockets can store up to M candies. The problem reduces to finding the smallest integer B such that B * (K * M) >= N. This is exactly the ceiling of N divided by (K * M). The formula (N + K*M - 1) / (K*M) using integer division yields the answer. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it)); M = int(next(it))
        cap = K * M
        bags = (N + cap - 1) // cap
        out.append(str(bags))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
